perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall pay such payment to Zogenix within [***] of receipt of the invoice.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor shall pay such payment to Zogenix within [***] of receipt of the invoice, unless otherwise mutually agreed in writing; however, in no case shall payment exceed 180 days from the invoice date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by allowing for potentially longer payment terms (up to 180 days) than the original '[***]' days, depending on a separate written agreement. This contradicts the original fixed payment timeline and creates uncertainty. This directly contradicts Section 7.6, which specifies interest accrual on late payments, as the determination of 'late' is now ambiguous. The contradiction stems from the added allowance of a different payment term that can be more flexible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that any payment due under this Agreement or the Supply Agreement is not made when due, the payment shall accrue interest from the date due at a rate per annum equal to [***] above the U.S. Prime Rate (as set forth in The Wall Street Journal, Eastern U.S. Edition) for the date on which payment was due, calculated daily on the basis of a 365-day year, or similar reputable data source; provided that in no event shall such rate exceed the maximum legal annual interest rate. The payment of such interest shall not limit a Party from exercising any other rights it may have as a consequence of the lateness of any payment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows for payment terms potentially exceeding the original '[***]' days, introducing flexibility. This directly contradicts the contradicted text regarding interest accrual on late payments, as the determination of 'late' becomes ambiguous due to the altered payment terms. The original contract specifies interest accruing on payments not made 'when due'. The modification permits a mutually agreed-upon payment schedule, potentially extending beyond the initial '[***]' days, creating direct inconsistency regarding when interest is applied. The change introduces uncertainty where the original text provided a clear deadline, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Zogenix shall be the marketing authorization holder of the Product in the Territory for the [***] following Regulatory Approval of the Product in the Territory. Thereafter, Zogenix shall transfer the marketing authorization for the Product in the Territory to Distributor within [***] following [***], unless delayed or prohibited by a Regulatory Authority or Applicable Law or otherwise agreed by the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Zogenix shall be the marketing authorization holder of the Product in the Territory for the [***] following Regulatory Approval of the Product in the Territory. Thereafter, Zogenix shall transfer the marketing authorization for the Product in the Territory to Distributor within [***] following [***], unless delayed or prohibited by a Regulatory Authority or Applicable Law. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, Zogenix may, at its sole discretion and without needing agreement from Distributor, retain the marketing authorization indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by granting Zogenix the unilateral right to retain the marketing authorization indefinitely, overriding the original obligation to transfer it to the Distributor after a specified period. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2.1, which grants Distributor the exclusive rights to maintain Regulatory Approval of the Product while Distributor is the MAH Party, as Zogenix's decision to retain the authorization would preclude Distributor from ever becoming the MAH Party. Additionally, it undermines the intended commercialization structure where Distributor would eventually control the regulatory aspects of the product, creating legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Grant of Rights to Distributor. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Zogenix hereby appoints Distributor, and Distributor accepts appointment, as the exclusive distributor (even as to Zogenix) of the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term, and grants to Distributor the exclusive rights to maintain Regulatory Approval of (while Distributor is the MAH Party), package, promote, market, offer for sale, sell, import and distribute the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term. The Parties agree that the rights granted in this Section 2.1 do not grant any right or license to, and Distributor shall not, supply, sell, offer for sale or otherwise dispose of or provide access to the Product in the Territory unless and until the Product obtains Regulatory Approval in the Field in the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The changed text gives Zogenix the right to retain marketing authorization indefinitely, which directly contradicts the distributor's exclusive rights to maintain regulatory approval while they are the MAH party, as stated in the contradicted text. This is a direct and clear conflict regarding control over regulatory aspects, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The original text obligated Zogenix to transfer the authorization, while the changed text allows Zogenix to retain it indefinitely, preventing the Distributor from ever becoming the MAH Party as intended by Section 2.1.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that either Zogenix or Distributor becomes aware of any infringement or threatened infringement by a Third Party in the Territory of any Zogenix Patents, it shall notify the other Party in writing to that effect.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that either Zogenix or Distributor becomes aware of any infringement or threatened infringement by a Third Party in the Territory of any Zogenix Patents, it may, at its sole discretion, notify the other Party in writing to that effect.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the obligation to notify the other party of patent infringement to a discretionary action ('may, at its sole discretion'). This conflicts with Section 9.3(b), which stipulates that "Each Party shall share with the other Party all information available to it regarding such alleged or threatened infringement by a Third Party." Making the initial notification discretionary hinders the mandatory information sharing in 9.3(b), because if the party does not decide to notify, the information will likely not be shared, creating legal uncertainty regarding the enforcement of patent rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Control of Action. Each Party shall share with the other Party all information available to it regarding such alleged or threatened infringement by a Third Party. Zogenix shall have the sole right, but not the obligation, to bring and control any action or proceeding with respect to alleged or threatened infringement by a Third Party in the Territory of any Zogenix Patent at Zogenix's cost and expense. If Zogenix elects to commence a suit to enforce the applicable Zogenix Patent against such infringements, then Distributor shall have the right to join such enforcement action, if permitted by Applicable Law, upon notice to Zogenix and be represented by a counsel of its own choice at its own cost and expense. Zogenix shall keep Distributor reasonably informed of any such actions or proceedings and consider in good faith any comments or requests made by Distributor, and the Parties shall cooperate and consult with each other in strategizing regarding any such action or proceeding, provided that Zogenix shall control and have the right to make all final decisions (regardless of whether or not Distributor is a party to such action or proceeding) regarding all matters in the preparation and conduct of any such action or proceeding. Each Party shall cooperate fully with the other Party with respect to actions or proceedings under this Section 9.3, including being joined as a party plaintiff in any such action or proceeding and providing access to relevant documents and other evidence and making its employees available at reasonable business hours, with the costs of such cooperation to be included as shared costs for actions or proceedings brought hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated notification of patent infringement, while the changed text makes it discretionary. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires 'Each Party shall share with the other Party all information available to it regarding such alleged or threatened infringement by a Third Party.' The change means a party could choose not to notify, thus preventing the sharing of information, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding enforcement of patent rights. The 'may' clause undermines the 'shall share' clause.
